EB-1A Visa Lawyers for Extraordinary Ability
The EB-1A visa is designed for individuals with extraordinary ability in sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics. If you’ve been recognized nationally or internationally for your work, this visa could be your direct path to a green card—no job offer needed.
- No employer sponsorship needed
- Over 20 years representing clients worldwide
- Thousands of U.S. green cards approved

## More than 20 years of Excellence in Immigration Law

## What Is the EB-1A Visa?
The Employment-Based First Preference Extraordinary Ability (EB-1A) visa is an immigration pathway for individuals with extraordinary ability in science, art, education, business, or athletics. To qualify, petitioners must show sustained national or international acclaim, provide documented recognition of their achievements, and intend to continue working in their field in the United States. They must also prove that their work will substantially benefit the country.
Our experienced EB-1A lawyers are here to guide you through every step and help build a strong, evidence-based petition that maximizes your chance of success.
- No work restrictions
- Allows self-petitioning
- A path to U.S. citizenship

## EB-1A Visa Requirements: Do You Qualify?
USCIS assesses EB-1A petitions in two steps. First, it determines whether the petitioner meets at least three of the 10 criteria or has a one-time achievement like a Nobel Prize. Then, it conducts a final merits determination to decide if the petitioner truly demonstrates extraordinary ability. Most petitioners qualify by meeting at least 3 of the 10 criteria listed below:
- National or International Awards
- Membership in Prestigious Associations
- Published Recognition
- Judging the Work of Others
- Original Contributions of Major Significance
- Scholarly Publications
- Artistic Exhibitions
- Leading or Critical Role
- Commercial Success in the Arts
For more details on each of these criteria, check out our in-depth look into EB-1A eligibility criteria .

## Frequently Asked Questions about EB-1A
USCIS defines “extraordinary ability” as a level of expertise indicating that the individual is one of the small percentage who have risen to the very top of their field. To qualify, petitioners must provide evidence of a widely recognized achievement, such as an Academy Award, Pulitzer Prize, or Nobel Prize, or, more commonly, meet at least three of the 10 evidentiary criteria outlined in the regulations.
Petitioners must also demonstrate sustained national or international acclaim and show that their work will substantially benefit the United States. For more details, check out our in-depth look into EB-1A eligibility criteria .
The final merits determination is the second step in the EB-1A adjudication process . At this stage, a USCIS officer reviews the petition in its entirety to determine eligibility. Using the “preponderance of the evidence” standard , the officer determine s if it is “probably true” or “more likely than not” that the petitioner is at the top of their field. A ll relevant evidence, including quality and quantity, is evaluated to determine whether the petitioner has sustained national or international acclaim. A high bar is applied, requiring petitioners to submi t clear, concise, and comprehensive evidence.
Yes, you can apply for the EB-1A visa without a job offer. The EB-1A classification is unique among visa categories under EB-1, as only EB-1A allows self-petitioning . This flexibility enables individuals with extraordinary abilities to seek permanent residence in the U.S. independently, without relying on a job offer or employer sponsorship. As the petitioner, you can submit your own application, providing evidence of your exceptional talent and achievements.
To apply for an EB-1A visa, first review the requirements and ensure you meet the eligibility criteria. Consult an EB-1A immigration attorney for guidance. Then, file Form I-140 with USCIS, providing evidence of extraordinary ability and intent to continue work in the U.S. This visa allows self-petitioning, eliminating the need for a job offer. Premium processing is available, shortening the processing time to 15 days . After approval, apply for a green card via Adjustment of Status or Consular Processing.
The EB-1A visa is one of several options for highly skilled individuals, including EB-1B, EB-2, and O-1 visas. EB-1A stands out for allowing self-petitioning and offering a direct path to a green card without going through the labor certification process . While O-1 and EB-1A requirements are similar, EB-1A has higher standards. Choosing the right category depends on individual circumstances, career goals, and need for permanent residency.
While not required, consulting an experienced EB-1A visa lawyer can be beneficial in navigating the complex application process. A lawyer can provide sound guidance, help avoid legal issues, and increase the chances of a successful application. With expert advice, you can effectively showcase your extraordinary abilities and achieve your goals in the United States.
